content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Fixed the remaining `ralph-burning-9ni.2.5` amendments in the filesystem adapter and CLI milestone repair flow. Plain milestone snapshot/journal reads now opportunistically acquire the milestone mutation lock and replay `.state-commit.json`, so a torn snapshot+journal commit is durably recovered even if no later writer arrives; the old masking fallback remains only for in-thread locked reads. In `run sync-milestone`, repaired attempts now derive their effective start time from the latest durable `run_resumed` event for the run instead of always using `run_started`, so resumed lineage repair emits the correct resume timestamp and preserves accumulated runtime metadata. I added regressions covering plain-read pending-commit recovery, partially visible snapshot repair, and resumed sync repair timing/duration behavior.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Added plain-read pending-state recovery in `src/adapters/fs.rs`, including thread-local mutation-lock tracking to avoid recursively reacquiring the milestone write lock during in-lock reads.
2. [Completed] Updated `src/cli/run.rs` to use the latest durable `run_resumed` timestamp as the repaired attempt start time for milestone sync, and added a resumed-run regression that checks the repaired lifecycle event timestamp and runtime metadata.
3. [Completed] Ran formatting and regression coverage for the touched areas, then verified the full library test suite still passes.

## Validation Evidence

- `cargo fmt --all` completed.
- `cargo test fs:: --lib` passed (18 tests).
- `cargo test cli::run::tests:: --lib` passed (14 tests).
- `cargo test --lib` passed (532 tests).

## Outstanding Risks

None identified.
